vulnerability.reference: Developers treat prompt-based uncertainty scores as reliable confidence estimates.
vulnerability.generated: Developers lack means to detect when confidence estimates diverge from true likelihoods under distribution shift.

impact.reference: Applications silently fail on edge cases while reporting high confidence.
impact.generated: LLM applications produce high-confidence errors, leading to unsafe automated decisions in deployment.

dose_response.reference: Prompt-based uncertainty detection fails under distribution shift, enabling miscalibrated high-confidence outputs that silently propagate through application pipelines, leading to harmful automated decisions.
dose_response.generated: Calibrated confidence estimates that may differ from true likelihoods, through developers integrating prompt-based uncertainty filters into LLM applications, triggers developers' lack of means to detect divergence under distribution shift, leading to high-confidence errors and unsafe automated decisions.
